objects.name: Analytic 1724
objects.description: Defender correlates an iOS-specific reduced-confidence chain where a supervised or managed device transitions from locked or inactive state to interactive or application-active state with weak evidence of expected user authentication, often accompanied by abnormal protected posture change, trust-state change, unexpected app wake, sensor use, or immediate downstream communication. Because direct visibility into lockscreen bypass mechanics on iOS is limited, the analytic prioritizes strong device-state effects and post-unlock behavior rather than pretending to observe the exact bypass method.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: iOS:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Passcode, biometrics, attention-aware authentication, or supervised-device lock policy changed in a way that weakens or alters the authentication boundary

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application wakes, becomes active, refreshes, or foregrounds immediately after locked or inactive state transition with weak recent user interaction

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Maximum allowed span between locked or inactive device state, suspicious app/service activity, and interactive transition.
Apps allowed to wake, foreground, or access protected resources near legitimate authentication events.
Whether the analytic should only apply to supervised devices with high-confidence MDM policy telemetry.
Time threshold for treating the transition as expected and user-driven.
User or device groups expected to use atypical enterprise lockscreen workflows, kiosk-like modes, or accessibility accommodations.
Apps expected to access camera or biometric-adjacent resources near the authentication boundary.
Expected destinations contacted immediately after legitimate app activation post-authentication.
Threshold for suspicious immediate outbound traffic after suspicious unlock-adjacent activity.
